name: Forbidden Threshold
spiceNote: Natural escalation point at intoxication — spice level 3+ appropriate here. The guilt beat must land before any physical resolution or the tension deflates.
antiPatterns: stating feelings directly instead of showing

name: Slow Burn Awakening
spiceNote: Spice level 1-2 can sustain this entire arc through sheer tension. The fracture is where suppression cracks — even a touch of a hand can carry enormous weight if the prior beats are earned.
antiPatterns: characters discussing their feelings before showing them

name: Power Exchange
spiceNote: The surrender moment is the spice peak — level 3-5 depending on what surrenders: body, will, or secret. The aftermath reckoning is non-negotiable; without it the power dynamic reads as abuse.

name: Forced Proximity
spiceNote: The accidental intimacy beat (shared warmth, tending a wound, sleeping in one bed) is where spice 2-3 content lives naturally. The external rupture should threaten the tentative connection just as it forms.
antiPatterns: forgetting hostility established in beat one
no real cost to the rupture

name: Rivals to Lovers
spiceNote: The desire-disguised-as-anger beat is the spice engine. Level 2-4 appropriate depending on how physical the argument becomes. Capitulation must feel earned by five prior beats or reads as unconvincing.

name: Second Chance
spiceNote: Past intimacy colors every touch with history. Spice level 2-4 — bodies remember what minds try to forget. The trust test must be genuine; if the character passes too easily, the reunion feels unearned.
antiPatterns: ignoring the reason for the original break

name: Secret Revealed
spiceNote: The revelation should arrive at maximum intimacy — the more trust built before, the harder it breaks. Spice 1-5 all serve this structure; the secret determines the register.
antiPatterns: protagonist conveniently absent during mounting clues

name: Danger Bonds
spiceNote: Survival strips social armor — spice 2-4 emerges naturally at vulnerability-exchanged beat. The complication when safety returns is critical; what felt necessary under threat must now be chosen.
antiPatterns: danger resolved before intimacy can form
characters bonding without real shared risk
returning to status quo once danger passes

name: Creature Hunger
spiceNote: The hunger metaphor carries all spice levels — level 1 is almost feeding, level 5 is everything. The transformation of hunger into something beyond appetite is the emotional payoff; do not skip it.
antiPatterns: no emotional cost to the feeding

name: Blood Oath
spiceNote: The binding itself can be spice level 2-4 depending on what the oath requires. The price revealed beat should land like a gut-punch; the reader must feel the protagonist had no good options.
antiPatterns: convenient loophole that negates the cost

name: Full Moon Fever
spiceNote: The primal self beat is spice level 3-5 territory. The human return must carry genuine weight — either shame, wonder, or grief. What the partner felt during the beast's dominance is the emotional question the chapter leaves open.
antiPatterns: beast with no distinct personality from the human
convenient full moon timing with no narrative setup

name: Fae Bargain
spiceNote: Fae bargains live by language — the most devastating price should be hidden in a word the protagonist glossed over. Spice 1-5 all work; the bargain's intimacy requirement sets the register.
antiPatterns: obvious trick the reader sees coming before the character
fae who explains the trick instead of letting it land

name: Dark Temptation
spiceNote: The rationalization beat is where voice must be precise — the protagonist must be convincing to themselves and to the reader. Spice 2-5 escalates with each compromise. The consequence cannot be deferred past the chapter.
antiPatterns: thin virtue at start makes temptation meaningless
protagonist who feels nothing about crossing the line

name: Forbidden Feast
spiceNote: A sensory arc — every beat should engage a different sense. One taste permitted is spice 2-3; feast consumed is 4-5. The new hunger revealed at the end should be emotionally deeper than the physical one satisfied.
antiPatterns: satiation that reads as resolution rather than deepening
new hunger that feels like a tacked-on twist

name: Ancient Claim
spiceNote: The claim demonstrated beat is where spice 3-4 lives — showing what the bond means physically is more powerful than explaining it. Resistance must cost the protagonist genuinely or the acknowledgment feels passive.
antiPatterns: claim that feels coercive without narrative critique
protagonist who changes position without sufficient story logic

name: Reluctant Protector
spiceNote: Protecting requires intimacy — this is the spice engine, level 1-3. The private acknowledgment beat should be internal monologue at its sharpest; what the protector admits to themselves is what they cannot yet say aloud.
antiPatterns: intimacy required without plausible story logic

name: Unraveling Control
spiceNote: The shattering is the spice event — level 3-5. But the reveal of what was being controlled is the emotional climax; the shattering is merely its vehicle. Both beats require equal page time.
antiPatterns: protagonist who reassembles control too quickly

name: Moonlit Surrender
spiceNote: The moon as structural device: what cannot happen in daylight becomes possible under it. Surrender witnessed by both parties — both characters must recognize what occurred. Dawn's reckoning cannot be soft.
antiPatterns: no real difference between the character who resisted and who surrendered

name: Venomous Affection
spiceNote: The one genuine moment of tenderness is the pivot on which the entire structure turns — it must be brief, unguarded, and immediately followed by retreat. Spice 2-4 lives in the indistinguishable beat.
antiPatterns: target who does not recognize the pattern
genuine tenderness that lasts too long
venom that reads as simple cruelty rather than complex feeling

name: Eternal Game
spiceNote: The game's rules are the story's architecture — they must be established clearly enough that breaking them has weight. Level 2-4 spice lives in the moment the player uses the game against its inventor. The consequence of breaking the rules is non-negotiable.
antiPatterns: rules too vague to create meaningful stakes
stakes that never exceed the original terms
consequence for broken rules that arrives conveniently or is avoided
